target.writeBoundary: Only counter.ps1 and test-counter.ps1 may change.

userConfirmedIntent.summary: Change the native counter demo behavior so the next value advances by two instead of one.
includedBehavior: Update the local runtime test expectation to match the new behavior.
excludedBehavior: Do not add a second module.

observedAnchors.meaning: The old active behavior increments by one.
The old runtime test expects 3 to advance to 4.

candidateMatch.recommendedShape: Modify the single function and the single local test expectation.
candidateMatch.whyThisShape: The behavior owner and runtime test are both explicitly bounded by the graph-source edges.
risksIfWrong: Changing README.md would turn a behavior dogfood into documentation-only proof.
Adding modules would hide whether graph-source boundaries are working.
Changing the test without changing behavior would create false evidence.

implementationPlan.expectedFlow: Change the counter step from +1 to +2 and update the local runtime test from 4 to 5.

forbiddenFlows.reason: This dogfood exists specifically to prove a tiny behavior change.
The selected graph-source boundary only opens the counter function and matching test.

build.summary: This demo has no build step.

remainingRisks: This behavior proof is intentionally tiny and does not represent a production application.
nextAllowedActions: Inspect graph delta for counter.ps1 and test-counter.ps1 only.
Use the result as the behavior-change dogfood baseline.
